Interpretation: The final statement of the appended SSLeay License "The licence and distribution terms for any publically available version or derivative of this code cannot be changed" normally must be interpreted as a copyleft clause, but there is no general consensus on this interpretation. Therefore, a recommendation on the compatibility of the OpenSSL license can only be given for selected cases.

Incompatibility of the PSF-2.0 license with the AGPL-3.0-only license is explicitly stated in the AGPL-3.0-only license checklist. Interpretation: The requirement of the PSF-2.0 license to provide a brief summary of the changes may definitely be seen as a further restriction the final license prohibits to impose, since it requires to describe the changes rather than only to mention the fact that changes were made and such brief summary must be included in source code and binary delivery. However, another interpretation may argue that the final license requires at least disclosure, though not always inclusion, of the complete corresponding source code which enables a sufficiently well trained engineer to extract the differences, understand the changes and derive a summary which then would be, at least partly, in line with the PSF-2.0 license requirement.
